message: Sync .beads: close bd-ezmts (COLLAPSE_RANGE), file INSERT_RANGE follow-up

 {"id":"bd-9g66o","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for unlink rmdir and rename","description":"Survey finding: ext4 fuse_e2e covers unlink, rmdir, and rename success/error behavior on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only refusal contract for these namespace removals and moves is not frozen. ffs-fuse already returns EROFS before dispatch on read-only mounts. Scope: add read-only ext4 probes for unlink, rmdir, and rename that assert exact EROFS and unchanged directory entries. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes read-only operator-visible refusal for unlink, rmdir, and rename.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:26:46.941132063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:04:36.974581164Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:04:36.974581164Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9i2v","title":"Audit and fix silent Result discards in btrfs write/unlink/truncate paths","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-20T02:08:00.606377012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T02:23:43.043605909Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T02:23:43.043583487Z","close_reason":"Propagated errors in btrfs write path (inline-to-regular conversion, device write, tree insert). Added warn! logging to 8 cleanup paths (truncate, unlink dir_item/dir_index/inode_ref, rename dotdot). All gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9jg91","title":"Freeze exact InodeLocation debug contract","description":"Tighten the InodeLocation debug-format test from substring checks to an exact golden assertion in crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T03:19:39.610078013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T03:24:20.383567951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T03:24:20.381393586Z","close_reason":"Added exact InodeLocation debug golden contract; verified with focused rch test plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["inode","testing-golden-artifacts"]}
+{"id":"bd-9mdyg","title":"[FUSE] fallocate FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE (shift right + insert hole)","description":"Follow-up to bd-ezmts: implement INSERT_RANGE = 0x20 in ext4_fallocate + a ffs_extent::insert_range helper (inverse of collapse_range).

INSERT_RANGE shifts every extent past offset right by length and grows file size by length, leaving an unallocated hole in [offset, offset+length). Both must be block-aligned. ext4 reference: ext4_insert_range in fs/ext4/extents.c.

Implementation sketch: ffs_extent::insert_range walks extents past the cut and re-inserts each at logical_block + count, then the caller bumps inode.size + nothing else (no allocation - the hole stays sparse until a write fills it).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:03:33.784406005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:03:33.784406005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9s4wt","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_alloc_bitmap target for bitmap operations","description":"ffs_alloc bitmap_* functions (bitmap_get, bitmap_count_free, bitmap_find_free, bitmap_find_contiguous) accept &[u8] but lack fuzz coverage.

Create fuzz target exercising all bitmap operations with boundary/invariant checks.

Acceptance: fuzz target passes cargo fuzz build.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:37:38.991826828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:31:01Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:31:01Z","close_reason":"Implemented fuzz_alloc_bitmap with bitmap get/count/find/set/clear model checks, seeded corpus, and remote cargo check/clippy verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9vft","title":"Fix dirty tracking bugs in ARC cache flush paths","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:24.234830612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:35.468375244Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:35.467685107Z","close_reason":"Fixed 2 bug classes in ARC cache flush:
(1) CRITICAL: flush_pending_evictions never called clear_dirty after writing blocks to device — dirty counts stayed inflated forever, causing false backpressure and potential infinite loop. Added clear_dirty loop after successful flush.
(2) HIGH: All 3 flush paths (flush_pending_evictions, flush_dirty_batch, flush_dirty) incorrectly restored already-persisted blocks as dirty when repair notification failed. Blocks were already on disk; re-marking them dirty caused unnecessary re-writes and count inflation. Changed to log-and-continue since repair notification is non-critical. 232 ffs-block tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block","cache","correctness"]}
 {"id":"bd-9xitm","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] ScrubDaemon not wired to ffs mount path; README's 'automatic recovery / no separate fsck' is aspirational at runtime","description":"# Reality-check finding — vision→runtime integration gap

## Vision claim (README.md:35, :838)

> TL;DR table, RaptorQ self-healing row: 'Corruption detected by checksums triggers automatic recovery. Stale-window SLO monitoring. **No separate fsck pass. No downtime.**'
>
> Corruption Recovery section: 'The scrub pipeline (`ffs-repair::pipeline`) continuously monitors block integrity and orchestrates automatic recovery.'

## Reality (runtime code)

`ScrubDaemon` is implemented in `crates/ffs-repair/src/pipeline.rs:695+` with `run_once`, `run_until_cancelled`, backpressure-aware scheduling, and evidence-ledger integration — all tested (bd-15c.5, bd-15c.6, bd-15c.11 all closed). But it is never started by any mount-path code:

- `crates/ffs-fuse/` — zero references to `ScrubDaemon`, `RepairPipeline`, or `start_scrub_daemon`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` — `ffs-repair` is not even a Cargo dependency of `ffs-core`
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs` mount dispatch at line 4160+ (`MountRuntimeMode::Standard` / `Managed` / `PerCore`) calls `mount_with_fuse` / `mount_managed` and returns — no daemon spawn

The 'automatic recovery' path is only exercised by the E2E test `e2e_survive_five_percent_random_block_corruption_with_daemon` inside `ffs-repair` itself, which instantiates the daemon directly in test scaffolding. There is no production call site that starts the daemon when a user runs `ffs mount`.

## Evidence

- `rg 'ScrubDaemon|start_scrub_daemon|RepairPipeline' crates/ffs-fuse/ crates/ffs-core/src/ crates/ffs-cli/` → zero matches
- `crates/ffs-fuse/Cargo.toml`, `crates/ffs-core/Cargo.toml`, `crates/ffs-block/Cargo.toml` → no `ffs-repair` dep
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs:4160-4180` mount dispatch: `mount_with_fuse(open_fs, mountpoint, read_write, allow_other, auto_unmount)?` — no repair/scrub setup

Additionally, the only read-path → repair hook in the codebase is the `RepairFlushLifecycle` trait (`crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs:512`), which fires AFTER writes flush (to refresh stale symbols). There is NO 'checksum failed during FUSE read → invoke RaptorQ decode' hook; `decode_group` is only called from within `ffs-repair` itself.

## Why this is a real vision gap

The README's self-healing promise is a top-line differentiator ('automatic recovery', 'no separate fsck pass', 'no downtime'). At runtime, users mounting via `ffs mount` get zero of this — checksum failures surface as I/O errors with no repair attempt, and corruption is not scanned in the background.

The components all exist and are tested; they're just not integrated into the runtime mount path.

## Fix direction

1. Add `ffs-repair` as a workspace dependency of `ffs-core` (so mount-time wiring can construct the pipeline).
2. Extend `OpenOptions` / `MountOptions` with `self_heal: SelfHealConfig { enabled, interval, budget, ... }`.
3. On mount, construct `ScrubDaemon` and spawn it under asupersync `Cx` so it honors the mount's cancellation tree (not `Cx::for_testing()` like the flush daemon does).
4. Add a read-path callback (mirror of `RepairFlushLifecycle` but for reads): when a block read surfaces a checksum failure, invoke `decode_group` to attempt repair; if successful, write back the repaired block and log an evidence-ledger event.
5. CLI: add `--self-heal / --no-self-heal` flag to `ffs mount` (default: enabled for `--rw`, disabled for RO since reads to bad blocks on RO-mounted images can't be fixed in place).
6. Document the runtime behavior change in README — move 'no separate fsck pass' from TL;DR to 'when self-heal is enabled' scope.

## Acceptance

- `ffs mount --rw --self-heal` starts the scrub daemon against the mounted image.
- A bit-flipped block on the mounted image, read through the FUSE path, is transparently repaired (evidence-ledger entry + corrected bytes returned).
- E2E test extended to drive the corruption scenario through `ffs mount` (not just via the in-process ScrubDaemon test harness).
- README either (a) stops claiming automatic recovery in the default mount path, or (b) ships with self-heal on by default once the integration is proven safe.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:24:35.381277309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:43.460999118Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:43.458709655Z","close_reason":"README+Limitations aligned with runtime reality in commit 7149da6: the TL;DR RaptorQ row now describes the CLI repair path that exists today (ffs repair / ffs fsck --repair) and explicitly defers background auto-scrub to V1.x; a new Limitations bullet documents that ScrubDaemon is implemented and tested via e2e_survive_five_percent_random_block_corruption_with_daemon but is not started by the ffs mount path. The full mount-path integration (new ffs-repair dep in ffs-core, SelfHealConfig on MountOptions, ScrubDaemon spawn under mount Cx, RepairReadLifecycle trait wiring on the read-path, --self-heal CLI flag) is tracked as follow-up feature bead bd-hoh5n with detailed acceptance criteria. Closing this reality-check finding: the vision-vs-reality mismatch is resolved on the doc side; the engineering work is captured in a separate, appropriately-scoped feature bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-evou","title":"Trim unused dependencies in ffs-dir/ffs-xattr/ffs-ondisk manifests","description":"Explorer audit found likely unused manifest deps: ffs-dir declares ffs-types/ffs-block/ffs-inode/asupersync but source uses only ffs_error + ffs_ondisk; ffs-xattr declares ffs-block but source does not use it; ffs-ondisk declares ffs-error but parser crate appears to use ParseError from ffs-types only. Remove unused deps, keep crate boundaries clean, and validate with rch-offloaded cargo check/clippy/fmt gates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T23:46:10.093749421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:34:22.114790888Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:34:22.114769979Z","close_reason":"Verified: manifests already trimmed (ffs-dir: only ffs-error+ffs-ondisk; ffs-xattr: ffs-types+ffs-error+ffs-ondisk; ffs-ondisk: ffs-types+crc32c+serde). cargo check passes cleanly via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ew490","title":"[HIGH] btrfs tamper-detection conformance still fails tree_block_csum validation at open","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failures:
- `btrfs_tree_block_checksum_tamper_detection_conforms` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:1103`
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` fails on the same root cause
- error: `open btrfs image: Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`

This is narrower than the umbrella conformance bead: the synthetic image used by the tamper-detection contract is still rejected before the actual tamper-detection assertion can run.

Acceptance:
- tamper-detection fixture opens successfully before the intentional corruption step
- `btrfs_tree_block_checksum_tamper_detection_conforms` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this btrfs checksum slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:26:03.678353852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:37:12Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:37:12Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","conformance","crc","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-ewdrq","title":"add mounted-path ext4 getxattr/listxattr size-probe and ERANGE coverage","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse already implements Linux xattr size-probe and ERANGE behavior via classify_xattr_reply/reply_xattr_payload, but fuse_e2e only exercises successful payload reads. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes for getxattr and listxattr with size=0 length discovery plus undersized-buffer ERANGE on real FUSE paths; assert exact reported lengths, exact errno, and no state mutation. Acceptance: ext4 E2E proves size-probe and too-small-buffer behavior for both getxattr and listxattr.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:07.763102031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-ezmts","title":"[FUSE] fallocate FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE + FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE","description":"Implement the non-default fallocate(2) modes that ext4 supports natively, routed through ffs-fuse + ffs-extent.

Context:
- FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE | FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE: deallocate the [offset, offset+len) range, replacing it with a hole. File size unchanged. ext4 implements via ext4_punch_hole -> ext4_ext_remove_space.
- FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE: remove [offset, offset+len) and shift everything past it left by len. Length must be block-aligned. ext4 implements via ext4_collapse_range -> ext4_ext_shift_extents.
- FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE: inverse of collapse — shift right and insert a hole.
- FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE: convert range to unwritten extents (reads return zero, no physical alloc).

ffs-extent already has truncate_extents and split_extent helpers. Need a punch_extents that splits + frees the middle range without touching the tail; a shift_extents that subtracts a constant from logical_block of all extents past the cut; and the FUSE FUSE_FALLOCATE opcode dispatcher needs to fan out by mode flags.

Reference: fs/ext4/extents.c::ext4_ext_remove_space, ext4_ext_shift_extents.

Acceptance:
- Test: write 32 KiB, punch_hole(8K, 8K), read back -> first 8K data, next 8K zeros, next 16K data.
- Test: write 4 file-aligned chunks, collapse_range(4K, 4K) -> chunk2 disappears, chunks 3+4 shift down, file_size shrinks by 4K.
- xfstests generic/{255, 316, 350} (punch + collapse + insert) pass on FUSE mount.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:27:16.730049133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:27:16.730049133Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-ezmts","title":"[FUSE] fallocate FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE + FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE","description":"Implement the non-default fallocate(2) modes that ext4 supports natively, routed through ffs-fuse + ffs-extent.

Context:
- FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE | FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE: deallocate the [offset, offset+len) range, replacing it with a hole. File size unchanged. ext4 implements via ext4_punch_hole -> ext4_ext_remove_space.
- FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE: remove [offset, offset+len) and shift everything past it left by len. Length must be block-aligned. ext4 implements via ext4_collapse_range -> ext4_ext_shift_extents.
- FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE: inverse of collapse — shift right and insert a hole.
- FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE: convert range to unwritten extents (reads return zero, no physical alloc).

ffs-extent already has truncate_extents and split_extent helpers. Need a punch_extents that splits + frees the middle range without touching the tail; a shift_extents that subtracts a constant from logical_block of all extents past the cut; and the FUSE FUSE_FALLOCATE opcode dispatcher needs to fan out by mode flags.

Reference: fs/ext4/extents.c::ext4_ext_remove_space, ext4_ext_shift_extents.

Acceptance:
- Test: write 32 KiB, punch_hole(8K, 8K), read back -> first 8K data, next 8K zeros, next 16K data.
- Test: write 4 file-aligned chunks, collapse_range(4K, 4K) -> chunk2 disappears, chunks 3+4 shift down, file_size shrinks by 4K.
- xfstests generic/{255, 316, 350} (punch + collapse + insert) pass on FUSE mount.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:27:16.730049133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:03:58.887387198Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T11:03:58.885533657Z","close_reason":"FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE shipped via ffs_extent::collapse_range (punch + tail-shift) + ext4_fallocate dispatch branch. 4 new ffs-core regressions: tail shifts and file shrinks correctly, unaligned offset/length surfaces EINVAL, range past EOF surfaces EINVAL, COLLAPSE_RANGE+KEEP_SIZE surfaces EINVAL. PUNCH_HOLE was already implemented. INSERT_RANGE deferred to follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
